We have modified the description of the "Thirsting Brush" Wheel of Destiny effect to make it easier to understand (the actual effect remains unchanged).
Please refer to the image description in the new post.
ah so it's the other awakener that gains the aliemus, not the wielder
The description make it seems like the other exalt get the crit, and the wielder get the aliemus
Because it is the wielder that consumes the collaborate stack
I guess the text is still confusing lol
<@1131790839291596841> Hello , for Ryker   Enhancement . Did you guys test it with normal Ryker or P12 Ryker with 2 WoD + 12 ??? 500 Sigil too much and hard to get it lol .

maybe with a more MtG style of text would be clearer, there's a reason MtG has a sorta dry text, but helps a lot to be clear:
"After the wielder uses Exalt, gain 1 stack of вАЬCollaborateвАЭ. On next non-wielder exalt, consume вАЬCollaborateвАЭ to increase its Crit Rate by 13/17/21/25%, the wielder gains Aliemus equal to 13/17/21/25% of his Aliemus Regen"
On next non-wielder exalt, during the turn
does it give crit rate for the turn or just to the exalt?
That is to see, what I meant is that the second exalt seems to have to be during that turn

The description seems pretty clear already
The wielder exalt create a stack
The next awakener that exalt consume it and get the 2 buffs
The second exalt don't get the buff
It get crit, but is the wielder that get the aliemus
The awakener that consume get the aliemus equal to x% of the wielder aliemus regen
Yeah, but is the wielder that get the stack
So is him that consume it
When the other exalt get the crit
that's the part that isn't certain, and could use some rewording
Plus, is more mechanically coherent that the support that build aliemus regen get more of it to exalt more, and the DPS get the crit

I wish my Uvhash to get more Aliemus
Let me cope
yeah, like it would trigger collaborate more often if the wielder gets the aliemus, but who knows

are you sure? it seemed clear to me from both the old and new wording that it goes:
- wheel holder gains collaborate after exalting.
- the next character to exalt consumes the collaborate on the wheel holder. that exalt's crit rate is increased.
- then, that character that consumed it also gains aliemus based *on the aliemus regen stat of the wheel holder*.

Maybe, but if that is the case, why do they repeat 2 times the frase and don't just say that the other one consumes it and get both?
They need to better specify on the wording

"After the wielder uses Exalt, gain 1 stack of вАЬCollaborateвАЭ. On next non-wielder exalt, consume вАЬCollaborateвАЭ to increase its Crit Rate by 13/17/21/25%, the exalting awakener gains Aliemus equal to 13/17/21/25% of wielderвАЩs Aliemus RegenвАЭ
I'm still not happy with the "the exalting Awakener", but can't find better wording yet
or just put вАЬand gain aliemus equal tooвА¶вАЭ
Yeah, we can just say that the next non-wielder exalt during the turn consume the stack and get the crit AND the aliemus
is the kit delayed because the translator was sick atm?
technically we still havenвАЩt reached the usual posting time for the kit yet

usually in 2 or more hours
could be sooner sometimes
